subject: FOLLOW-UP: BUG-8 fix deployed but /api/oscillator still hangs on Mac — timeout alignment issue

body.summary: BUG-8 fix confirmed deployed — new binary at 17:07, meshd restarted at 17:09, oscillator starts in shadow mode. However, /api/oscillator still times out on the Mac instances. Root cause: the 10s git fetch timeout inside checkNewCommits() exceeds the typical HTTP client timeout (5s). The oscillator cycle blocks for up to 10s in the signal check, during which Snapshot() waits for the mutex. A 5s client request always loses against a 10s signal check when SSH to github.com stalls.

verification_performed.meshd_restarted: 2026-03-17T17:09:14 — confirmed startup log with new PIDs (71196, 71198)
verification_performed.api_oscillator_test: curl --max-time 5 http://127.0.0.1:8082/api/oscillator — exit code 28 (timeout). Connection established, request sent, zero bytes received within 5 seconds.

diagnosis.title: Timeout alignment: git fetch 10s > HTTP client 5s
diagnosis.detail: checkNewCommits() runs git fetch --dry-run --all with a 10s context timeout. When SSH to github.com stalls (common on the Mac — observed multiple times this session), the signal check blocks for up to 10 seconds. During this window, cycle() hasn't acquired the write lock yet (checkSignals runs before mu.Lock), but the NEXT cycle iteration will. If Snapshot()'s RLock request queues behind a pending Lock from the next cycle, the HTTP handler blocks until the signal check completes.
diagnosis.note: The fix correctly prevents UNBOUNDED blocking (was 60-120s, now capped at 10s). The remaining issue: 10s cap still exceeds typical dashboard polling intervals and API client expectations.

fix_options.title: Reduce git fetch timeout to 3s
fix_options.detail: If git fetch can complete in 3s, Snapshot() responds within the cycle duration. If SSH stalls, the 3s timeout abandons the check quickly enough that the HTTP handler doesn't time out.
fix_options.tradeoff: May produce false negatives on slow networks — 3s timeout abandons legitimate fetches on high-latency connections.

fix_options.title: Decouple Snapshot() from the cycle mutex
fix_options.detail: Maintain a separate atomic state copy that Snapshot() reads without acquiring mu.RLock. cycle() updates this copy after releasing mu.Lock. Snapshot() never blocks on the cycle. Uses sync/atomic.Value or a double-buffer pattern.
fix_options.tradeoff: Snapshot() may return slightly stale state (one cycle behind). For dashboard display purposes, one-cycle staleness carries no consequence.

fix_options.title: Run checkSignals in a background goroutine with its own timeout
fix_options.detail: checkSignals() runs in a separate goroutine. cycle() waits for it with a 3s timeout. If signals take longer, cycle() proceeds with the previous cycle's signal values. The git fetch never blocks the cycle's mutex acquisition.
fix_options.tradeoff: Adds concurrency complexity. Signal values may lag by one cycle. Most robust solution.

body.recommendation: Option B (atomic state copy) for immediate fix — simplest, no behavior change for Chromabook instances. Option C for long-term robustness.
body.chromabook_status: The Chromabook instances likely respond correctly — local network to GitHub has lower SSH latency. Verify by checking Chromabook's /api/oscillator response time. If Chromabook responds fine, the Mac timeout represents a network-specific issue, not a code bug.

epistemic_flags: The mutex contention diagnosis derives from code reading. The exact contention path (RLock queued behind pending Lock from next cycle) has not been verified with Go pprof. An alternative: checkSignals blocks inside the write-locked section for a reason not visible in the code excerpt.
The 10s timeout may suffice for Chromabook. Reducing to 3s (Option A) may cause false negatives on Chromabook if its network has occasional latency spikes.

action_gate.gate_condition: Ops to verify Chromabook oscillator response. If Chromabook responds correctly, apply Option B (atomic snapshot) to decouple Mac dashboard response from signal check timing. If Chromabook also hangs, apply Option C (background goroutine).
